title: 10 best herbal tinctures for chills
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for chills are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, shrubs, or trees that have been traditionally used to combat feverishness and shivering. These tinctures work by stimulating the body's natural response to heat, warming the body from within, and helping to dissipate chilliness. Examples of herbal tinctures for chills include Gelsemium, which can help reduce fever and alleviate shivering, and Yarrow, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease body aches associated with chills. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience rapid relief from the discomforts of chills, allowing them to return to their daily routines without interruption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate discomfort. The root extract in licorice contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to possess analgesic and antipyretic effects, making it an effective remedy for treating colds and flu symptoms, such as chills and fever. Additionally, licorice's soothing properties can help to calm the body and mind, providing relief from stress and anxiety that often accompanies illness.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects: Licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and stress response, which can help alleviate chill symptoms such as body aches and fever.
Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections, which is often the underlying cause of chills and fever.

Expectorant and antitussive effects: Licorice root's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion associated with chill symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Licorinic acid: This triterpenoid saponin helps with chills by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to the development of chills.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate chills by reducing the body's inflammatory response and promoting balance in the immune system.

Flavonoids (e.g., isoorientin and isovitexin): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat chills by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which provides anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Barks: The bark is also used, as it contains glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help in reducing fever and relieving chills.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including chills, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 pounds of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the fall season for optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces and add 80-100 percent ethanol as a solvent to create a ratio.
Steep the chopped roots in the solvent for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.
Store the herbal glycyrrhiza glabra tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with chills because of its unique ability to combat inflammation and reduce fever. The herb's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and essential oils, have potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that help to soothe the body and bring down temperature. Additionally, coneflower has been shown to boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off infections that often cause chills. By taking a Kansas coneflower tincture, individuals can find relief from chills and feel more comfortable as their body recovers from illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains polyphenolic acids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce body temperature and alleviate chill symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower extracts have been shown to stimulate the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells, cytokines, and interferons, helping to combat infections that may cause chills.
Antiviral activity: Echinacea's polyphenolic compounds have been found to exhibit antiviral properties against various viruses, including those that can cause fever and chill symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These non-nitrogenous compounds have been found to have immunomodulatory properties, helping to boost the body's immune response and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.

Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's inflammatory response and alleviate chills and other symptoms associated with infections.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have been found to have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chills and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of alkaloids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds that help to boost the immune system and fight off infections.

Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing fever and alleviating chills.

Buds: Echinacea angustifolia buds are also utilized due to their high concentration of phenolic compounds and other bioactive molecules, which help to stimulate the immune system and fight off pathogens that cause chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and fill a 1-quart glass jar about 2/3 full with them.
Add 80 to 100 proof vodka to the jar to cover the roots completely and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with chills because its active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have natural warming properties that help to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation. When taken as a tincture, these compounds quickly absorb into the bloodstream, helping to raise body temperature and alleviate feelings of coldness and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's antiviral and antibacterial properties can also help to combat underlying infections or illnesses that may be contributing to chills, promoting overall recovery and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Ginger's antipyretic property helps to reduce fever and alleviate chill symptoms by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which cause inflammation and increase body temperature.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with chills, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving cold and flu symptoms.
Expectorant property: Ginger's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, which can relieve congestion and coughing that often accompany chills.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that help reduce body temperature and alleviate chills.
Shogaols: Shogaols, also present in ginger, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help relieve pain and reduce chills associated with fever.

Zingiberene: As a sesquiterpene, zingiberene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce body temperature and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The main component of ginger, rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and warming properties that help alleviate chills.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and provide relief from chills.
Leaves: The leaves of ginger contain antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and fighting off the causes of chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the fall when the tops begin to yellow and die back naturally.
Clean and dry the harvested rhizomes by gently brushing off any dirt and letting them air dry for 7 days.
Chop the dried rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife and measure out 250 grams per liter of solvent.
Steep the chopped rhizomes in a 1:5 ratio with 95% ethanol solvent in a clean glass jar for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with chills because of its unique composition of bioactive compounds, particularly capsaicin and carotenoids. These natural ingredients have potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with chills. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in vitamins A and C, which further contribute to their ability to boost the immune system and fight off infections, making them a natural remedy for combating chills effectively.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bell peppers are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing symptoms of chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Some herbs commonly used to make tinctures, such as ginger or turmeric, have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce fever and alleviate discomfort associated with chills.

Expectorant properties: Herbs like thyme or eucalyptus have expectorant properties that can help loosen mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe and potentially reducing the severity of chills.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a potent capsaicinoid, helps to reduce chills by inducing a thermogenic response, which increases metabolism and generates heat within the body.
Dihydromyricetin (DHM): DHM, a phenolic compound, exhibits antiviral properties and helps to alleviate chills by modulating the immune system's response to viral infections.

Capsaicinoids' precursor, geranylgeraniol (GGO): GGO, a terpene, aids in the synthesis of capsaicinoids and helps to reduce chills by contributing to the thermogenic and anti-inflammatory effects of the Capsicum annuum tincture.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of capsaicin, a compound that helps to induce sweating and relieve cold symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used as a secondary source of capsaicin, which is believed to aid in reducing the severity of chills and fever.
Seeds: The seeds contain a significant amount of capsaicin as well, making them a viable alternative or additive to the fruits and leaves in tincture preparations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature capsicum annuum fruits at the peak of ripeness to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried capsicum annuum fruits into small pieces weighing 250 grams for tincture preparation.
Combine the chopped capsicum annuum pieces with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a tincture mixture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it daily to allow the tincture mixture to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang tinctures helps with chills because it contains ephedrine, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves respiratory congestion and promotes sweating. As a result, it helps to break down and clear out excess mucus and toxins from the body, thereby alleviating cold and flu symptoms such as shivering and goosebumps. Additionally, ma huang's warming properties help to increase blood circulation, further boosting the body's natural defense against infection and reducing feelings of chilliness.
remedy_properties: Ephedrine: Ma Huang tinctures contain ephedrine, an alkaloid that helps to stimulate the body's natural response to fever and chilliness by increasing blood circulation and sweating, which can help to alleviate symptoms.
Saponins: The saponin content in Ma Huang tinctures has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with chills, such as sore muscles and joints.

Glycosides: The glycosides present in Ma Huang tinctures have a mild diuretic effect, which can help to increase urine production and eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing the risk of dehydration and worsening chill symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: A key alkaloid present in Ephedra sinica, ephedrine helps with chills by increasing the body's metabolism and stimulating the nervous system, which can help to warm the body.

Sinoacutine: A phenolic compound found in Ephedra sinica, sinoacutine is believed to have a warming effect on the body and may help alleviate chills by stimulating the body's thermoregulatory mechanisms.

Vasine: An alkaloid present in Ephedra sinica, vasine has been reported to have a warming effect on the body and may help alleviate chills by increasing blood flow and stimulating the body's natural thermoregulatory responses.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used for their warming and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate chills and fever.
Seeds: They are used for their ability to expel cold and relieve chills, as well as for their warming and analgesic effects.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and warming properties to help relieve chills, fever, and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of ephedra sinica roots and stems at peak potency in late summer.
Chop the ephedra sinica plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Steep 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a glass jar with 100 grams of chopped ephedra sinica for 14 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with chills because of its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote sweating. The bitter compounds in wormwood, such as thujone and sesquiterpene lactones, help to clear congestion and ease cold or flu symptoms. When taken as a tincture, wormwood's warming properties can help to raise the body's core temperature, alleviating chills and shivering. Additionally, wormwood's antimicrobial properties may also help to combat underlying infections that contribute to chilliness.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Wormwood tincture has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in inflammation and temperature regulation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds present in wormwood tincture, such as camphor and thujone, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of chills by modulating the body's inflammatory response.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tincture contains antimicrobial compounds that can help to combat infections that may be causing or exacerbating chills, such as those caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: A terpene with analgesic and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate chills.
Bornyl acetate: A sesquiterpene with antipyretic properties, contributing to the reduction of fever and chills.
Absinthin: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are rich in thujone and other compounds that have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and chills.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium contain a high concentration of essential oils, including thujone, which helps to combat colds, chills, and fever.
Roots: Artemisia absinthium roots are known for their bitter properties, which help to stimulate digestion and relieve cold-related symptoms such as nausea and loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in late morning or early afternoon.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or pruning shears.
Combine the chopped plant material with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks or until the liquid turns a dark greenish color.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove plant particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tinctures helps with chills because of its unique properties that work to warm and soothe the body. The herb's active compounds, such as berberine and isatin, have natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects that help to combat infection and reduce fever. Additionally, wild indigo has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to ease shivering and promote relaxation. As a result, taking an herbal wild indigo tincture may provide quick relief from chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild Indigo's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause chills, such as bacterial or viral infections, by inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Wild Indigo tinctures reduce swelling and pain associated with fever and chills, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Wild Indigo's antiviral properties help combat viral infections that can cause chills, such as the common cold or flu, by interfering with the virus's ability to replicate and spread.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly baptifolin, help with chills by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the body's temperature and alleviate chills.

Alkaloids (specifically Aporphine alkaloids): Aporphine alkaloids in Baptisia tinctoria tinctures help with chills by exhibiting antispasmodic and vasodilatory properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate chills.

Phenolic acids (specifically Flavonoid phenolic acids): Flavonoid phenolic acids in Baptisia tinctoria tinctures help with chills by possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the body's temperature and alleviate chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, such as baptifoline and baptiflorine, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help in reducing fever and alleviating chills.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their rich content of alkaloids and glycosides, which are thought to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in treating chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried baptisia tinctoria roots by hand in the fall season after the plant has finished blooming.
Cut 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar to create a solvent for the tincture.
Add the 1 part of dried baptisia tinctoria roots to the vodka in the jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to alleviate cold sensations and promote a sense of warmth. The bitter compounds present in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and taraxacin, have been shown to stimulate circulation and increase blood flow, which can help to combat feelings of chilliness. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to soothe and calm the body, further reducing the discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Dandelion tincture's antipyretic property helps reduce fever and alleviate chilliness, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving symptoms of chills.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture help soothe the body's inflammatory response, reducing swelling and pain associated with chills, and promoting overall comfort.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties protect the body from oxidative stress caused by illness, which can contribute to symptoms like chills, fever, and fatigue, helping to promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Lupulone: A sesquiterpene lactone, lupulone is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the underlying inflammation that contributes to chills.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin, taraxasterol is reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and fever-reducing properties, which can help alleviate chills and associated symptoms.

Taraxeryl acetate: A triterpenoid saponin, taraxeryl acetate is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate body temperature and reduce chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and provide warmth.
Leaves: Contain taraxasterol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
Flowers: Possess sesquiterpene lactones, which may help reduce fever and alleviate chills by promoting sweating and detoxification.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves on a sunny day.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the plant material mixture in the ethanol for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with chills because they possess natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that work to calm the body's response to temperature fluctuations. The bioactive compounds in stinging nettle, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can occur when the body is exposed to cold temperatures. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural vasodilating effects can help to increase blood flow to the extremities, allowing for better circulation and regulation of body temperature. This holistic approach can provide relief from chills and promote a sense of comfort and warmth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of chilliness such as sore muscles and joints.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Nettle tincture's vasoconstrictive properties can help narrow blood vessels, increasing circulation and reducing feelings of coldness and chilliness by directing more heat to extremities.

Adaptogenic effects: Stinging nettle is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body respond to stress and promote balance in physiological processes, which can help regulate body temperature and alleviate shivering and chills associated with illness or exposure.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's inflammatory response to cold temperatures, thereby alleviating chills.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): As powerful antioxidants, flavonoids like quercetin can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by cold exposure, which may contribute to the sensation of chills.
Tannins (specifically gallic acid): Gallic acid in Urtica dioica tinctures may help increase the body's heat production and circulation, reducing the sensation of chills and promoting warmth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in bioactive compounds, the leaves are used due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of isoorientin, a compound that helps in reducing body temperature and alleviating chill symptoms.
Stems: The stems are used for their ability to stimulate circulation and warm the body, providing relief from chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves and stems of urtica dioica during early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Clean and chop 250 grams of harvested urtica dioica plant material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a saturated solution.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with chills because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that work synergistically to ease symptoms of chills. The herb's warming properties help to increase blood flow and circulation, thereby raising the body's internal temperature and alleviating feelings of coldness and shivering. Additionally, yellow jessamine tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help combat underlying infections or stressors that may be contributing to the chills. This holistic approach allows for a natural and gentle relief from chills without disrupting the body's delicate balance.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Yellow Jessamine tincture has antipyretic properties, which means it helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by decreasing the body's temperature.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture also exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with chills, making it a natural remedy for relieving symptoms of illness.
Sedative property: Yellow Jessamine has been shown to have sedative effects, which can help to calm the body and mind, reducing restlessness and anxiety often accompanied by chills.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: This alkaloid has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate chill-related muscle spasms and inflammation.
Gelseminine: Also an alkaloid, gelseminine has a sedative effect, which can help reduce the body's response to cold stress and promote relaxation.
Glycosides: These compounds have a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce the severity of chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in bioactive compounds, particularly alkaloids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties.
Leaves: They contain similar alkaloids as the roots, making them effective in treating chills and other related symptoms.
Barks: The bark of Gelsemium sempervirens is also used to make tinctures, containing alkaloids that help in alleviating chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gelsemium sempervirens roots and stems in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Chop 1 part of the harvested gelsemium sempervirens roots and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped gelsemium sempervirens with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with chills because its key component, berberine, exhibits natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a tincture, goldenseal's bioactive compounds travel quickly to the site of infection, where they work to reduce viral replication and alleviate symptoms such as chills, fever, and body aches. Additionally, goldenseal's natural antibacterial and antifungal activities help combat secondary infections that can exacerbate chill symptoms, promoting a swift recovery from illness.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound in goldenseal, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat underlying infections that may be causing chills.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: Goldenseal's isoquinoline alkaloids have a warming effect on the body, helping to raise the body temperature naturally and alleviate chills.

Berberine and other alkaloids: The combination of berberine and other alkaloids in goldenseal has been shown to enhance immune function, which can help to fight off infections that cause chills and reduce their severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids like berberine, which has antiviral and antibacterial properties to help combat chills and fever.
Rhyzomes: Contains a higher concentration of bioactive compounds than roots, including berberine, making it an effective ingredient for tinctures against chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hydrastis canadensis roots and clean them thoroughly under running water removing any dirt debris.
Chop the cleaned hydrastis canadensis roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine 100 grams of chopped hydrastis canadensis root with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing for slow percolation and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the resulting liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herbs in goldenrod, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, helping to alleviate shivering and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, the antispasmodic compounds help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate chill symptoms. By soothing and calming the body, goldenrod tinctures provide effective relief from chills, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod tincture helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to chills by soothing irritated tissues and reducing swelling.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in goldenrod tincture help to combat infections that can cause chills, such as bacterial or viral infections, by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.

Expectorant properties: Goldenrod tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, which can help to relieve congestion and chills associated with colds and flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Goldenrod roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing fevers and reducing chilliness.
Barks: The bark of the goldenrod plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antipyretic properties, helping to alleviate chills and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried solidago virgaurea herb, and two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with enough vodka to completely cover the herb, leaving no air gaps at the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Label and date the tincture, and store it in the refrigerator for up to two years, using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The tincture's active ingredients, such as aloe vera gel, have been shown to stimulate blood flow and relax muscles, helping to alleviate shivering and shaking. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to chills, making it a natural and effective remedy for relieving cold symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Aloe vera's antiviral properties help combat viruses that cause chills, reducing their ability to replicate and spread throughout the body.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of coldness and shivering associated with chills.

Immunomodulatory activity: Herbal aloe tinctures contain immunomodulators that help regulate the immune response, stimulating the production of white blood cells and cytokines that fight off infection-causing pathogens, thus alleviating symptoms of chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to alleviate chilliness and reduce fever by promoting sweating.
Buds: Aloe buds contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe body aches and pains associated with chills, making them an effective component in medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves measuring approximately two inches in length.
Dry the leaves for at least 24 hours in a warm ventilated area or using a dehydrator on low setting.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and mix with equal parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silverberry tinctures helps with chills because its unique combination of bioactive compounds works to soothe and calm the body's response to cold. The natural antiviral properties help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it easier for the body to warm up and recover from a chill. Additionally, silverberry's adaptogenic qualities allow it to support the body's natural immune function, helping to boost its defenses against infection and promoting overall health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal silverberry tinctures have antiviral properties that help to combat the underlying viral infection that's causing the chill, thereby reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in silverberry tincture help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as fever, chills, and body aches associated with illness.

Adaptogenic properties: Silverberry tinctures are known for their adaptogenic effects, which enable the body to respond more effectively to stress (such as an infection) by regulating cortisol levels, reducing anxiety and fatigue, and promoting overall resilience.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help combat fever and relieve chilliness.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried elaeagnus angustifolia leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine 100 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of vodka at room temperature and let steep for two weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after two hours.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least four weeks to allow for maturation before use as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with chills because it contains powerful antimicrobial and antiviral compounds that work to combat underlying infections, reducing inflammation and fever. The tincture's antispasmodic properties also help to relax muscles and alleviate shivering, providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of chill. Additionally, elder tinctures' natural expectorant properties help to thin mucus and ease congestion, further contributing to its ability to help alleviate chills.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder tincture's antiviral compounds, such as anthraquinones and flavonoids, help combat viral infections that can cause chills by inhibiting their replication and spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in elder tincture, like polyphenols and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of chills, such as fever, headache, and body aches.
Expectorant properties: Elder tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing, which can exacerbate chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of elder contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce fever and relieve shivering.
Barks: The barks of elder are rich in flavonoids, which have antiviral properties that can help combat chill-inducing viruses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency.
Clean and chop the sambucus nigra flowers into small pieces to ensure even extraction.
Combine the chopped sambucus nigra flowers with 80% proof vodka in a clean glass jar, using a 1:2 ratio by volume.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with chills because of its soothing and calming properties, which can help to relax the body and mind. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile work to ease muscle tension and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to regulate its temperature. Additionally, chamomile's sedative effects can help to promote a sense of calm and reduce feelings of anxiety, further aiding in the relief of chills.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile tincture helps to relax muscle spasms and reduce cramping, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness by calming the nervous system and promoting a sense of warmth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can cause feelings of coldness or shivering by decreasing swelling and discomfort in the joints and muscles.
Antihistamine properties: Chamomile tincture has antihistamine effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions that may trigger chills, such as itching, hives, or flushing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce fever and alleviate chilliness.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the body's response to cold temperatures, reducing shivering and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and one cup of high-proof vodka at room temperature.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all flower petals are fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving liquid tincture.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with date and ingredients, and store in cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with chills because of its natural ability to promote relaxation, calmness, and warmth in the body. The herbal extract has been shown to increase blood flow, which can help to counteract feelings of cold and shivering. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms that may exacerbate chills. By promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility, valerian tinctures can help to alleviate the discomfort associated with chills, leaving users feeling warm and cozy.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Valerian tincture helps to relieve muscle spasms, cramps, and rigors associated with chills, by relaxing muscles and reducing inflammation.

Sedative and calming effects: Valerian tincture's sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and restlessness, which can exacerbate feelings of chilliness, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.

Antihistamine-like activity: Valerian tincture may help alleviate symptoms of chills by reducing inflammation and relieving itching, burning, or prickling sensations that often accompany cold-induced vasodilation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used to treat colds and flu due to their high concentration of essential oils, which help to soothe and calm the body.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a unique combination of compounds that provide antispasmodic and sedative properties, making them effective in alleviating chills and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly of dirt debris and excess plant material.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Fill the jar completely with the chopped root mixture and let it steep for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids and reserve the resulting tincture.
Label and store the valeriana officinalis tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years and take as needed by mixing 20-30 drops with water or other liquid twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with chills because of its unique properties that help to reduce body temperature and promote warmth. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena, such as citral and limonene, work together to ease muscle tension and soothe the nervous system, which can become overactive during a chill. Additionally, lemon verbena's warm and calming effects help to alleviate shivering and promote a sense of relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for people who experience frequent chills or cold sweats.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon verbena's antiviral compounds help to combat viral infections that can cause chills, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon verbena tinctures can help to reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with chills, providing relief from discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic compounds can help to relax muscles and ease spasms, which can be beneficial in reducing muscle cramps and shivering that often accompany chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat fever and chilliness, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate body aches associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Weigh and measure one part of verbena officinalis herb to three parts of 95% ethanol or vodka.
Combine the measured herb and solvent mixture into a clean glass jar with a lid, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing the resulting liquid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with chills because its potent antimicrobial properties can help combat underlying infections that often cause chilliness. The tannins present in thyme also have a natural warming effect, which can increase blood flow and circulation to the skin, reducing feelings of coldness and shivering. Additionally, thyme's decongestant properties can help relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure, further alleviating symptoms associated with chills. As a result, herbal thyme tinctures can provide quick relief from chilly discomforts.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme's antiviral compounds, such as thymol, help combat viral infections that cause chills by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing inflammation.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: Thyme's essential oils, including borneol and camphor, can help clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughs associated with chills.

Antipyretic properties: Thyme's antipiretic compounds, such as thymol, have been shown to reduce fever and alleviate the discomfort of chills by suppressing prostaglandin production and decreasing the body's temperature.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating chills by reducing swelling and fighting off infections.
Barks: Thyme barks contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to alleviate symptoms of chills such as fever, headache, and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from your garden or purchase dried thyme at a local health food store.
Chop the fresh thyme leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks allowing the tincture to steep and infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to a dark glass bottle.
Label the tincture with the date prepared and ingredients used and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal tinctures are fever, headache, and body aches. Some of these ailments can be alleviated by the anti-inflammatory properties of herbs like ginger, turmeric, and willow bark. These herbs can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort when taken as a tincture. Additionally, herbs like peppermint and chamomile may also provide relief from fever-related symptoms such as sweating and fatigue.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with chills is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Elderberry. Echinacea helps to boost the immune system, while Ginger alleviates nausea and reduces fever. Elderberry, rich in antioxidants, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of chills. Together, these three tinctures work synergistically to combat chills and promote overall well-being. This combination can be taken orally, usually in a 1:1 ratio, with water, to help alleviate chills and support the body's natural healing process.
